nodes.name: Best-Selling Albums
nodes.description: The highest-selling albums in music history

items.prompt: Which 1982 album produced by Quincy Jones yielded seven top-10 singles including 'Billie Jean' and 'Beat It,' becoming the best-selling album of all time?
items.explanation:
Released November 30, 1982 on Epic Records; produced by Quincy Jones
Best-selling album of all time with estimated 70 million copies sold worldwide
Yielded seven singles, all of which reached the top 10 on the Billboard Hot 100
'Billie Jean' music video broke MTV's color barrier — the channel had rarely played Black artists before
Won a record eight Grammy Awards at the 1984 ceremony
Title track's 14-minute music video, directed by John Landis, premiered as a theatrical event
Spent 37 weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200; returned to #1 after the artist's death in 2009

items.prompt: Which 1980 hard rock album was the first release by its Australian band after the death of original singer Bon Scott, going on to sell approximately 50 million copies?
items.explanation:
Released July 25, 1980 on Albert/Atlantic Records
First album after vocalist Bon Scott died of acute alcohol poisoning in February 1980
Brian Johnson (from Geordie) replaced Scott; the album title and all-black cover are a tribute to Scott
Second-best-selling album of all time with approximately 50 million copies sold worldwide
Certified 25× Platinum in the U.S. alone
Produced by Robert John 'Mutt' Lange; contains 'Hells Bells,' 'You Shook Me All Night Long,' and the title track
Recorded at Compass Point Studios in Nassau, Bahamas in just five weeks

items.prompt: Which 1973 concept album about life's pressures — featuring a prism-and-rainbow cover by Storm Thorgerson — spent nearly 1,000 weeks on the Billboard 200?
items.answer: The Dark Side of the Moon
items.explanation:
Released March 1, 1973 on Harvest Records; estimated 45 million copies sold worldwide
Spent nearly 1,000 cumulative weeks on the Billboard 200 — more than any other album in chart history
Iconic prism cover art designed by Storm Thorgerson and Hipgnosis
Concept album exploring themes of conflict, greed, time, death, and mental illness
Recorded at Abbey Road Studios; innovative use of synthesizers, tape loops, and multitrack recording
Contains no singles in the traditional sense, but 'Money' (in 7/4 time) and 'Time' received heavy radio play
Engineer Alan Parsons later launched his own career as the Alan Parsons Project
items.cardBack: The Dark Side of the Moon

items.prompt: Which 1992 film soundtrack featured a Dolly Parton cover that became the best-selling physical single of all time, with total album sales reaching approximately 45 million copies?
items.explanation:
Soundtrack to the 1992 film starring the lead vocalist and Kevin Costner
Sold approximately 45 million copies worldwide, making it the best-selling soundtrack album ever
'I Will Always Love You' — originally a 1973 Dolly Parton country hit — spent 14 weeks at #1
That single became the best-selling physical single by a female artist in history
Album spent 20 weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200
Also includes 'I'm Every Woman' (Chaka Khan cover), 'I Have Nothing,' and 'Run to You'
Won three Grammy Awards including Album of the Year and Record of the Year

items.prompt: Which 1977 album, written and produced by Jim Steinman, was rejected by every major label for years before becoming one of the best-selling records of all time at 44 million copies?
items.explanation:
Released October 21, 1977 on Cleveland International/Epic Records
Written and produced by Jim Steinman; estimated 44 million copies sold worldwide
Rejected by every major label for roughly two and a half years before release
Todd Rundgren produced and played guitar on the album after labels refused to fund it
Blends Springsteen-style rock with Wagnerian operatics and motorcycle imagery
Spent over 500 weeks on the UK Albums Chart — one of the longest runs in British chart history
Title track runs 9:51; 'Paradise by the Dashboard Light' is a theatrical duet with Ellen Foley

items.prompt: Which 1976 compilation — the first album ever certified Platinum by the RIAA — collected hits from its band's first four albums and has sold approximately 44 million copies?
items.explanation:
Released February 17, 1976 on Asylum Records; compiles singles from the band's first four studio albums
First album ever to receive RIAA Platinum certification (February 24, 1976, one week after release)
First album certified Quadruple Diamond (40 million U.S. units) in January 2026
Includes 'Take It Easy,' 'Witchy Woman,' 'Desperado,' 'One of These Nights,' and 'Best of My Love'
Approximately 44 million copies sold worldwide
Briefly held the title of best-selling album in U.S. history, trading the record with 'Thriller'
All five singles on the album reached the top 10 on the Billboard Hot 100

items.prompt: Which 1997 country-pop crossover album — featuring 'You're Still the One' and 'Man! I Feel Like a Woman!' — became the best-selling studio album by a female artist?
items.explanation:
Released November 4, 1997 on Mercury Nashville; produced by Robert John 'Mutt' Lange
Best-selling studio album by a female artist with approximately 40 million copies sold worldwide
Best-selling country album of all time
Produced 12 singles — every track except one was released as a single
Lange (also the artist's then-husband) co-wrote every song on the album
Dominated both country and pop charts, spending 50 weeks in the top 10 of the Billboard 200
'You're Still the One' earned two Grammys; 'Man! I Feel Like a Woman!' won Best Female Country Vocal

items.prompt: Which 1977 album was recorded while two couples in the band were splitting up, producing hits like 'Go Your Own Way,' 'Dreams,' and 'The Chain'?
items.explanation:
Released February 4, 1977 on Warner Bros. Records; approximately 40 million copies sold worldwide
Recorded during simultaneous breakups: Lindsey Buckingham and Stevie Nicks, John and Christine McVie
Personal turmoil fueled confessional songwriting — 'Go Your Own Way' was Buckingham's breakup song to Nicks
'Dreams' became the band's only #1 single on the Billboard Hot 100
Won the Grammy for Album of the Year at the 1978 ceremony
Spent 31 weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200
'The Chain' — the only song credited to all five members — became the BBC's Formula 1 theme from 1978 onward

items.prompt: Which 1971 album has no official title, band name, or text on its cover, yet contains a track widely considered the greatest rock song ever recorded?
items.explanation:
Released November 8, 1971 on Atlantic Records; approximately 37 million copies sold worldwide
Officially untitled — no band name, album title, or text on the cover, only four symbols (one per member)
Jimmy Page's symbol resembles 'ZoSo,' giving the album one of its common nicknames
Contains 'Stairway to Heaven,' which was never released as a single but became the most-requested FM radio song of the 1970s
Also includes 'Black Dog,' 'Rock and Roll,' and a reworking of Memphis Minnie's 'When the Levee Breaks'
The cover's front image — an old man with a bundle of sticks — was a painting found in an antique shop in Reading
Certified 24× Platinum in the U.S. by the RIAA

items.prompt: Which 1977 film soundtrack spawned 'Stayin' Alive' and 'How Deep Is Your Love,' defining the disco era and selling approximately 40 million copies?
alternates: Saturday Night Fever: The Original Movie Sound Track
items.explanation:
Released November 15, 1977 on RSO Records; approximately 40 million copies sold worldwide
Soundtrack to the 1977 film starring John Travolta as Tony Manero
Dominated by the Bee Gees, who wrote and performed five of the album's biggest hits
'Stayin' Alive,' 'How Deep Is Your Love,' and 'Night Fever' each reached #1 on the Billboard Hot 100
Spent 24 consecutive weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200
Became both the symbol and the commercial peak of the disco era
Also features tracks by Yvonne Elliman ('If I Can't Have You,' #1) and Tavares

items.prompt: Which 1969 album features a famous crosswalk photograph on its cover, along with tracks like 'Come Together' and 'Here Comes the Sun'?
items.explanation:
Released September 26, 1969 on Apple Records; approximately 35 million copies sold worldwide
Cover photo — the four members walking across the zebra crossing outside the studio — is one of the most imitated images in music
The crossing on Abbey Road in St John's Wood, London, was granted Grade II listed status in 2010
Side two features a 16-minute medley of eight songs stitched together, ending with 'The End'
George Harrison's 'Something' and 'Here Comes the Sun' are considered his finest contributions to the group
Last album recorded by the band (though *Let It Be* was released later in 1970)
Produced by George Martin; Geoff Emerick engineered

items.prompt: Which 1987 debut album — featuring 'Welcome to the Jungle' and 'Sweet Child O' Mine' — became the best-selling debut in U.S. history?
items.explanation:
Released July 21, 1987 on Geffen Records; approximately 35 million copies sold worldwide
Best-selling debut album in U.S. history, certified 18× Platinum by the RIAA
Took over a year to reach #1 on the Billboard 200 (August 1988), propelled by MTV airplay of 'Sweet Child O' Mine'
Original Robert Williams cover art (a robot assaulting a woman) was replaced after retailers refused to stock it
'Welcome to the Jungle,' 'Paradise City,' and 'Sweet Child O' Mine' all reached the top 10
Recorded with producer Mike Clink at Rumbo Recorders, Take One, and other studios in Los Angeles
Band lineup: Axl Rose, Slash, Izzy Stradlin, Duff McKagan, Steven Adler

items.prompt: Which 2011 album yielded 'Rolling in the Deep' and 'Someone Like You,' sweeping six Grammy Awards including Album of the Year?
items.explanation:
Released January 24, 2011 on XL/Columbia Records; approximately 31 million copies sold worldwide
Named after the artist's age at the time of writing; themes of heartbreak and regret
Swept six Grammy Awards at the 2012 ceremony, tying the record for most wins by a female artist in one night
'Rolling in the Deep' spent seven weeks at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100; 'Someone Like You' also reached #1
Spent 24 non-consecutive weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200
Became the first album to sell 3 million copies in a single year in the UK
Produced primarily by Paul Epworth and Rick Rubin; blends soul, pop, and blue-eyed soul

items.prompt: Which 1995 album marked its Canadian artist's pivot from teen dance-pop to alternative rock, featuring 'You Oughta Know' and 'Ironic' and selling 33 million copies?
items.explanation:
Released June 13, 1995 on Maverick/Reprise Records; approximately 33 million copies sold worldwide
The artist had previously released two dance-pop albums in Canada (1991, 1992) that went nowhere internationally
Co-written and produced with Glen Ballard in a stripped-down, raw style that defined mid-'90s alt-rock
'You Oughta Know' (featuring Flea and Dave Navarro) was the breakthrough single; its frank anger shocked radio
'Ironic' became a pop-culture touchstone — and a perennial debate about whether any of its examples are actually ironic
Won five Grammy Awards in 1996 including Album of the Year
Adapted into a Tony-winning Broadway musical in 2019

items.prompt: Which 1991 album — known for its baby-in-a-swimming-pool cover — knocked *Dangerous* off #1 on the Billboard 200, signaling the mainstream arrival of grunge?
items.explanation:
Released September 24, 1991 on DGG/Sub Pop; approximately 30 million copies sold worldwide
Knocked Michael Jackson's *Dangerous* off #1 on the Billboard 200 on January 11, 1992
Sold only 6,000 copies in its first week; reached #1 within four months, driven by MTV's rotation of 'Smells Like Teen Spirit'
Baby-in-pool cover shot by Kirk Weddle; the underwater baby (Spencer Elden) later sued the band over the image
Produced by Butch Vig at Sound City Studios in Van Nuys, California
Widely credited with killing hair metal and bringing grunge/alternative rock into the mainstream
The band's frontman died in April 1994 at age 27, making this the group's commercial peak
